# Disentangling supports {#sec-disentangle}

A piecewise-constant control separates the $N$ overlapping input supports into pairwise-disjoint
regions of the sphere, by steering each measure's *barycenter* into its own direction and then
shrinking its geodesic hull around it. This disentanglement is the heart of the construction behind
Theorems 1.1 and 1.2: once the supports are disjoint, each measure can be clustered and matched
independently.

::: {.from-paper}
**From the original paper** (Geshkovski, Rigollet, Ruiz-Balet, §3, p. 14).

"We show that flows generated by self-attention can disentangle measures with overlapping supports—it
actually suffices to consider $\mathbf B\equiv 0$." Set $\mathbb Q_1^{d-1} := \mathbb S^{d-1}\cap(\mathbb R_{>0})^d$
and
$$\mathsf v[\mu](t,x) = \mathbf P_x^\perp\!\big(\mathbf V(t)\,\mathbb E_{\mu(t)}[z] + \mathbf W(t)(\mathbf U(t)x + b(t))_+\big). \tag{3.1}$$
The whole section uses this reduced field (3.1): a perceptron term $\mathbf W(\mathbf Ux+b)_+$ and a
barycentric attention term $\mathbf V\,\mathbb E_{\mu}[z]$, both projected onto the tangent space.
:::

## Transport to the orthant {#node-orthant}

::: {.from-paper}
**From the original paper** (Geshkovski, Rigollet, Ruiz-Balet, Lemma 3.2, p. 15).

Suppose $T>0$ and $\mu_0^i\in\mathscr P(\mathbb S^{d-1})$ with $\bigcup_i\operatorname{supp}\mu_0^i\subsetneq\mathbb S^{d-1}$.
There exists a piecewise constant $\mathbf W:[0,T]\to\mathscr M_{d\times d}(\mathbb R)$, having at most
one switch and satisfying
$$\|\mathbf W\|_{L^\infty((0,T);\mathscr M_{d\times d}(\mathbb R))}\le C/T$$
for some $C=C(N)>0$, such that for any $i\in[\![1,N]\!]$ the solution $\mu^i$ to (1.3)–(1.2) with data
$\mu_0^i$ and $\mathbf V\equiv\mathbf B\equiv\mathbf U\equiv 0$, $b\equiv\mathbf 1$, satisfies
$\operatorname{supp}\mu^i(T)\subset\mathbb Q_1^{d-1}$.

**Proof.** *(proof sketch; full proof p. 15.)* Pick a direction $\omega\in\mathbb S^{d-1}\setminus\bigcup_i\operatorname{supp}\mu_0^i$
not filling the sphere and set $\mathbf W_1\mathbf 1=-\omega$. The characteristics solve
$\dot x=-\mathbf P_x^\perp\omega$, so $\frac{d}{dt}\langle x,\omega\rangle=-1+\langle x,\omega\rangle^2<0$
off $\{\pm\omega\}$, hence by some $T_0$ all supports lie in $B(-\omega,\pi/8)$. Pick
$\alpha\in\mathbb Q_1^{d-1}$ with $d_g(-\omega,-\alpha)>\pi/8$ (so $-\alpha$ is off every support) and on
$[T_0,T]$ take $\mathbf W_2\mathbf 1=\alpha$: now $\frac{d}{dt}\langle x,\alpha\rangle=1-\langle x,\alpha\rangle^2\ge0$
drives every point into a prescribed small cap around $\alpha\in\mathbb Q_1^{d-1}$. One switch; the
norm bound follows by time-rescaling the two phases. $\square$
:::

::: {.our-work}
**Our formalization.** [open]{.badge .open}

The authors' construction (attributed to them) is the one switch above: a first phase
$\mathbf W_1\mathbf 1=-\omega$ contracts every support toward $-\omega$ through the gate-free dynamics
$\frac{d}{dt}\langle x,\omega\rangle=-(1-\langle x,\omega\rangle^2)$ — the projector identity of leaf
**L1** — and a second phase $\mathbf W_2\mathbf 1=\alpha\in\mathbb Q_1^{d-1}$ drives them into a small
cap inside the orthant. We give a **type-correct Lean statement against the axiom layer**
(`measureFlow`, `switches`), expressing "support inside $\mathbb Q_1^{d-1}$" measure-theoretically as
`μ (orthant d)ᶜ = 0`. It is a `sorry` stub: we state it, we do **not** claim a proof; its discharge
rests on the continuity-equation flow axiom.

```lean
/-- The open positive orthant `Q₁^{d-1} = 𝕊^{d-1} ∩ (ℝ_{>0})^d`, as a subset of `ℝ^d`. -/
def orthant (d : ℕ) : Set (Eucl d) := {x | ∀ i, 0 < x i}

/-- "The support of `μ` is contained in `S`", expressed measure-theoretically as `μ(Sᶜ) = 0`. -/
def supportedIn (μ : Measure (Eucl d)) (S : Set (Eucl d)) : Prop := μ Sᶜ = 0

/-- **Lemma 3.2** (transport into the orthant). One parameter switch moves the measure into
`Q₁^{d-1}`. Stub (`sorry`): `math.open`. -/
theorem lemma_3_2 (μ : Measure (Eucl d)) (T : ℝ) (hT : 0 < T) :
    ∃ θ : Params d, switches θ ≤ 1 ∧ supportedIn (measureFlow θ T μ) (orthant d) := by
  sorry
```

[`Statements/MidLevel.lean`](https://github.com/aquemy/measure-to-measure-transformers/blob/main/MeasureToMeasure/Statements/MidLevel.lean){.lean-src}
:::

## Shrinking a hull to its barycenter direction {#node-shrink}

::: {.from-paper}
**From the original paper** (Geshkovski, Rigollet, Ruiz-Balet, Lemma 3.3, p. 16; proof App. B.2, p. 33).

Let $\mu_0^i\in\mathscr P(\mathbb Q_1^{d-1})$ be such that $\mathbb E_{\mu_0^i}[x]$ is not colinear with
$\mathbb E_{\mu_0^j}[x]$ for $i\ne j$. Then for any $T>0$, $\varepsilon>0$, and
$\nu_0\in\mathscr P(\mathbb Q_1^{d-1})$ such that $\mathbb E_{\nu_0}[x]$ is colinear with
$\mathbb E_{\mu_0^i}[x]$, there exists a piecewise constant $\theta:[0,T]\to\Theta$ having at most
$O(d\cdot N)$ switches such that
$$\operatorname{conv}_g\operatorname{supp}\nu(T)\cup\operatorname{conv}_g\operatorname{supp}\mu^N(T)\subset B\!\left(\mathbb E_{\mu_0^j}[z]\,\Big/\,\big\|\mathbb E_{\mu_0^j}[z]\big\|,\ \varepsilon\right)$$
and $\mu^i(T)=\mu_0^i$ for $i\ne j$, where $\mu^i,\nu$ denote the unique solutions to (1.3)–(3.1)
corresponding to data $\mu_0^i,\nu_0$, and parameters $\theta$.

**Proof.** *(proof sketch; full proof App. B.2, p. 33.)* Three steps. **(1) Isolating** $\mu_0^N,\nu_0$:
with $\mathbf W\equiv 0$ and $\mathbf V(t)=\sum_k\alpha_k\alpha_k^\top\mathbf 1_{[T_{k-1},T_k]}$, where
$\{\alpha_k\}$ is an orthonormal basis of $\operatorname{span}(\mathbb E_{\mu_0^N}[z])^\perp$, the
barycenter ODE (B.9)
$\frac{d}{dt}\langle x,\alpha_1\rangle=\langle\mathbb E_{\mu(t)}[x],\alpha_1\rangle(1-\langle\alpha_1,x\rangle^2)$
sends $x(t)\to\pm\alpha_k$, concentrating $\operatorname{supp}\mu(T_{d-1})$ near
$\bigcup_k B(\pm\alpha_k,C_k\varepsilon_k)\subset\mathbb S^{d-1}\setminus\mathbb Q_1^{d-1}$ (flow
$\Psi_1$, fixing $\mu_0^j$ for $j\le N-1$). **(2) Clustering:** a separating half-space
$\langle a,x\rangle+\underline b$ with $\mathbf U=\mathbf 1a^\top$, $\mathbf W_2\mathbf 1=\mathbb E_{\mu_0^N}[x]$
drives both supports into $B(\mathbb E_{\mu_0^N}[x]/\|\mathbb E_{\mu_0^N}[x]\|,\delta)$ (flow $\Psi_2$).
**(3) Flow reversal:** $\Phi_{\mathrm{fin}}:=\Psi_1^{-1}\circ\Psi_2\circ\Psi_1$ returns $\mu_0^i$ to
itself for $i\le N-1$ and clusters $\nu,\mu^N$ within $B(\,\cdot\,,C_T\delta)$; pick $\delta$ small. $\square$
:::

::: {.our-work}
**Our formalization.** [open]{.badge .open}

The authors' three-step argument (App. B.2) hinges on the **barycenter ODE (B.9)**, which is exactly
our machine-checked leaf **L6** (`barycenter_hasDerivAt_inner`): along the attention field,
$\frac{d}{dt}\langle x,\alpha\rangle = c\,(1-\langle x,\alpha\rangle^2)$ with strict increase for $c>0$,
which is what drives each point to $\pm\alpha$ and lets the support be concentrated into an
$\varepsilon$-ball around the barycenter direction. We state the conclusion type-correctly against the
axiom layer: some $\theta$ concentrates the flow into `Metric.ball α ε`. It is a `sorry` stub stated
against `measureFlow`; the analytic content (the three flows $\Psi_1,\Psi_2,\Psi_1^{-1}$) lives in the
flow axiom.

```lean
/-- **Lemma 3.3** (shrink a measure's hull toward its barycenter direction). For any tolerance the
measure can be concentrated into a small ball around some direction `α`, with `O(dN)` switches.
Stub (`sorry`): `math.open`. -/
theorem lemma_3_3 (μ : Measure (Eucl d)) (T ε : ℝ) (hT : 0 < T) (hε : 0 < ε) :
    ∃ (θ : Params d) (α : Eucl d), supportedIn (measureFlow θ T μ) (Metric.ball α ε) := by
  sorry
```

[`Statements/MidLevel.lean`](https://github.com/aquemy/measure-to-measure-transformers/blob/main/MeasureToMeasure/Statements/MidLevel.lean){.lean-src}
:::

## Making barycenters non-colinear {#node-make-noncolinear}

::: {.from-paper}
**From the original paper** (Geshkovski, Rigollet, Ruiz-Balet, Lemma 3.4, p. 16; proof App. B.3, p. 35).

Let $T>0$ and let $\mu_0,\nu_0\in\mathscr P(\mathbb Q_1^{d-1})$ be two different measures such that
$\mathbb E_{\mu_0}[x]=\gamma_1\mathbb E_{\nu_0}[x]$ for some $\gamma_1\in(0,1]$.

1. If $\gamma_1=1$, then, setting $\mathbf V\equiv 0$, there exist $\mathbf W,\mathbf U\in\mathscr M_{d\times d}(\mathbb R)$
and $b\in\mathbb R^d$ such that the solutions $\mu,\nu$ to (1.3)–(3.1) corresponding to $\mu_0,\nu_0$ and
these parameters, satisfy $\mathbb E_{\mu(T)}[x]\ne\mathbb E_{\nu(T)}[x]$. Moreover the
Lipschitz-continuous and invertible flow map $\Phi^T:\mathbb S^{d-1}\to\mathbb S^{d-1}$ induced by the
characteristics satisfies $\Phi^T(x)=x$ for $x\in\mathbb S^{d-1}\setminus(\operatorname{conv}_g\operatorname{supp}\mu_0\cup\operatorname{conv}_g\operatorname{supp}\nu_0)$. (3.2)

2. If $\gamma_1\ne1$, then, setting $\mathbf B\equiv 0$, there exist piecewise-constant
$(\mathbf V,\mathbf W,\mathbf U)$ and $b$ with at most $2$ switches such that
$\mathbb E_{\mu(T)}[x]\ne\gamma_2\,\mathbb E_{\nu(T)}[x]$ for all $\gamma_2\in\mathbb R$ (non-colinear).

**Proof (Part 1).** *(proof sketch; full proof App. B.3, p. 35.)* Since $\mu_0\ne\nu_0$, there is an
open ball $\mathcal B\subset\operatorname{supp}\mu_0\cup\operatorname{supp}\nu_0$ with
$\mu_0(\mathcal B)\ne\nu_0(\mathcal B)$. There exists $x^*\in\mathcal B$ with
$$\mu_0(\mathcal B)x^*+\int_{\mathbb S^{d-1}\setminus\mathcal B}x\,\mu_0(\mathrm dx)\ \ne\ \nu_0(\mathcal B)x^*+\int_{\mathbb S^{d-1}\setminus\mathcal B}x\,\nu_0(\mathrm dx),$$
for otherwise $x^*=\frac1{\mu_0(\mathcal B)-\nu_0(\mathcal B)}\int_{\mathbb S^{d-1}\setminus\mathcal B}x(\nu_0-\mu_0)(\mathrm dx)$
would be forced constant over all of $\mathcal B$ — impossible. Taking such $x^*$, a perceptron block
$\mathbf U=-\mathbf 1a^\top$, $b=R\mathbf 1$ ($a,R$ the center and radius of $\mathcal B$), and
$\mathbf W\mathbf 1=x^*$, by Lemma B.2 pushes $\mu(T)$ to $\mu_0(\mathcal B)\delta_{x^*}+\mu_0(\cdot\setminus\mathcal B)$;
continuity of the expectation in $\mathsf W_2$ yields $\mathbb E_{\Phi_\#\mu_0}[x]\ne\mathbb E_{\Phi_\#\nu_0}[x]$,
with $\Phi(x)=x$ off $\mathcal B$. $\square$
:::

::: {.our-work}
**Our formalization.** [open]{.badge .open}

In the $\gamma_1=1$ (perceptron) case the authors locate a ball $\mathcal B$ on which $\mu_0,\nu_0$
differ in mass, then argue that **some $x^*\in\mathcal B$ makes the barycenters differ** — for if every
$x^*$ gave equality, $x^*$ would be pinned to one value on the whole ball, which no map can be. That
"no map is constant on a nonempty open ball" core is our machine-checked leaf **L10**
(`exists_ne_in_ball`). We state the $\gamma_1=1$ conclusion type-correctly: equal input
barycenters force the existence of a $\theta$ whose output barycenters differ. `sorry` stub against the
axiom layer. (The attention case $\gamma_1\ne1$, at most two switches, is the companion stub
`lemma_3_4_part2`.)

```lean
/-- **Lemma 3.4, Part 1** (`γ₁ = 1` case). If two measures have equal barycenters, a constant
parameter makes the barycenters differ. Stub (`sorry`): `math.open`. The self-contained pigeonhole
core is leaf L10. -/
theorem lemma_3_4_part1 (μ ν : Measure (Eucl d)) (T : ℝ) (hT : 0 < T)
    (hbar : barycenter μ = barycenter ν) :
    ∃ θ : Params d, barycenter (measureFlow θ T μ) ≠ barycenter (measureFlow θ T ν) := by
  sorry
```

[`Statements/MidLevel.lean`](https://github.com/aquemy/measure-to-measure-transformers/blob/main/MeasureToMeasure/Statements/MidLevel.lean){.lean-src}
:::

## Disentanglement {#node-disentanglement}

::: {.from-paper}
**From the original paper** (Geshkovski, Rigollet, Ruiz-Balet, Proposition 3.1, pp. 15-17).

Let $T>0$ and $\mu_0^i\in\mathscr P(\mathbb Q_1^{d-1})$. There exists a piecewise constant
$\theta:[0,T]\to\Theta$, having at most $O(d\cdot N)$ switches, such that for all $i\in[\![1,N]\!]$, the
solution $\mu^i$ to (1.3)–(3.1) with data $\mu_0^i$ and $\theta$ satisfies
$$\operatorname{conv}_g\operatorname{supp}\mu^i(T)\cap\operatorname{conv}_g\operatorname{supp}\mu^j(T)=\varnothing\quad\text{if } i\ne j.$$

**Proof.** *(proof sketch; full proof pp. 16-17.)* Induction on $N$; the base case $N=1$ is trivial.
Assume the first $N-1$ measures already have pairwise-disjoint geodesic hulls (3.3) and let $\mu_0^N$
be arbitrary. Since $\operatorname{supp}\mu_0^i\subset\mathbb Q_1^{d-1}$, (3.3) implies
$\mathbb E_{\mu_0^i}[x]$ is not colinear with $\mathbb E_{\mu_0^j}[x]$ for $i\ne j\le N-1$. If
$\mathbb E_{\mu_0^N}[x]$ is non-colinear with all of them, one application of Lemma 3.3 (small
$\varepsilon$) shrinks $\mu^N$'s hull until it separates. Otherwise $\mathbb E_{\mu_0^N}[x]$ is colinear
with $\mathbb E_{\mu_0^i}[x]$ for at most one $i$ (WLOG $i=N-1$), and four phases of $[0,T]$ finish it:
(1) $[0,T/4]$ Lemma 3.3 peels $\mu^N,\mu^{N-1}$ off the rest; (2) $[T/4,T/2]$ Lemma 3.4 Part 1 makes
$\mathbb E_{\mu^{N-1}}[x]\ne\mathbb E_{\mu^N}[x]$ while (3.2) keeps the others fixed; (3) $[T/2,3T/4]$
Lemma 3.4 Part 2 makes the two barycenters non-colinear; (4) $[3T/4,T]$ Lemma 3.3, now applicable to
all $N$ measures, separates them. Total $O(d\cdot N)$ switches. $\square$
:::

::: {.our-work}
**Our formalization.** [open]{.badge .open}

We state the headline controllability conclusion of the authors' induction type-correctly against the
axiom layer: there is a $\theta$ whose solution map renders the family's supports pairwise disjoint,
recorded by the opaque predicate `DisjointSupports`. It is a `sorry` stub — the four-phase induction,
the $O(dN)$ switch count, and the geodesic-hull nesting all rest on the flow / $\operatorname{conv}_g$
axioms, so we do not claim a proof.

```lean
/-- The measures in the family have pairwise disjoint (geodesic-convex-hull) supports. -/
axiom DisjointSupports {N : ℕ} (ν : Fin N → Measure (Eucl d)) : Prop

/-- **Proposition 3.1** (disentanglement). There is a piecewise-constant `θ` whose solution map
renders the supports of the family pairwise disjoint. Stub (`sorry`): status `math.open`. -/
theorem prop_3_1 (hd : 3 ≤ d) {N : ℕ} (μ₀ : Fin N → Measure (Eucl d)) (T : ℝ) (hT : 0 < T) :
    ∃ θ : Params d, DisjointSupports (fun i => measureFlow θ T (μ₀ i)) := by
  sorry
```

[`Statements/MainResults.lean`](https://github.com/aquemy/measure-to-measure-transformers/blob/main/MeasureToMeasure/Statements/MainResults.lean){.lean-src}
:::

::: {.strengthen}
**What we strengthen.** *(finding F2.)* The induction above opens by asserting that **disjoint
geodesic hulls imply non-colinear barycenters** —
$$\operatorname{conv}_g\operatorname{supp}\mu_0^i\cap\operatorname{conv}_g\operatorname{supp}\mu_0^j=\varnothing\ \Longrightarrow\ \mathbb E_{\mu_0^i}[x]\ \text{not colinear with}\ \mathbb E_{\mu_0^j}[x]$$
— **without proof**. Our adversarial review flagged this as the one genuine rigor gap (finding F2). It
is now the machine-checked leaf **L11** (`barycenter_noncolinear_of_disjoint_hull`, see the
[Self-contained leaves](#sec-leaves) section) for the empirical regime of Theorem 1.1 / restricted
Theorem 1.2, and **L11$'$** (`barycenter_noncolinear_of_disjoint_hull_general`) for arbitrary
probability measures via Mathlib's `Convex.integral_mem`. The barycenter, a nonnegative average of
support points, lies in $\operatorname{cone}(\operatorname{supp})$; colinearity would put a common
normalized direction in *both* hulls, contradicting disjointness. The headline `prop_3_1` stays
`[open]{.badge .open}` only because it still rests on the flow / $\operatorname{conv}_g$-nesting
axioms — not on this step. (Validated numerically by experiment E3.)

[`Leaves/BarycenterNonColinear.lean`](https://github.com/aquemy/measure-to-measure-transformers/blob/main/MeasureToMeasure/Leaves/BarycenterNonColinear.lean){.lean-src}
:::
